Motion To Stay Proceedings Under The Federal Arbitration Act Or, Alternatively, Under The Courts Inherent Authority, Pending Interlocatory Appeal Of Order Denying Petition To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar Apr-28-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT SOLANA LABS, INC.'s Motion To Stay Proceedings Under The Federal Arbitration Act Or, Alternatively, Under The Courts Inherent Authority, Pending Interlocutory Appeal Of Order Denying Petition To Compel Arbitration.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant Solana Labs, Inc.'s motion to stay proceedings in this court pending its appeal of the order denying arbitration is granted.

The parties agreed that the Federal Arbitration Act, 9 U.S.C. sections 1 et seq., would "substantively and procedurally govern" their contracts. (Wolfe Dec., Ex. 1 part 1, pp. 113, 151 [page references are to the pagination of the pdf documents filed in the court's electronic register of actions].) Thus, the parties expressly selected the FAA's procedural rules. The FAA requires trial courts to stay litigation pending appeal of a decision denying arbitration. (See Coinbase, Inc. v. Bielski (2023) 599 U.S. 736.)

Even if the FAA did not apply, the court would exercise its discretion to stay the proceedings pending appeal. The court has inherent power to stay proceedings to ensure economy of effort for the court and the parties before it. (See OTO, L.L.C. v. Kho (2019) 8 Cal.5th 111, 141.) Plaintiff Coefficient Group Holding Limited offers no reason why it would be prejudiced by a stay, while Solana if forced to proceed to litigation while its appeal is pending would lose the arbitral forum that the appeal seeks to vindicate. (See Coinbase, supra, at p. 743 ["[C]ontinuation of proceedings in the district court largely defeats the point of the appeal."] [internal quotation marks and citation omitted].)

Coefficient argues that the motion seeks reconsideration of the court's prior order denying an order compelling arbitration and denying a stay pending arbitration. The court concludes otherwise; the relief Solana seeks is a stay pending appeal, not a stay pending arbitration. Because the relief sought is different, this motion is not governed by the procedural requirements of CCP section 1008.

Order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)
